links.title: Compute Where it Counts: Self Optimizing Language Models

primary_link.title: Compute Where it Counts: Self Optimizing Language Models

papers.summary: Efficient LLM inference research has largely focused on reducing the cost of each decoding step (e.g., using quantization, pruning, or sparse attention), typically applying a uniform computation budget to every generated token. In practice, token difficulty varies widely, so static compression can over-compute on easy steps and under-compute on hard ones. We study dynamic budget allocation for autoregressive decoding: learning how much computation to spend per token from within a single model.
papers.summary_zh: 高效能的 LLM inference 研究主要集中於降低每個 decoding 步驟的成本（例如，使用 quantization、pruning 或 sparse attention），通常對每個生成的 token 應用統一的計算預算。實際上，token 的難度差異很大，因此靜態壓縮可能在簡單的步驟上過度計算，而在困難的步驟上計算不足。我們研究 autoregressive decoding 的動態預算分配：學習如何在單一模型內部決定每個 token 應花費多少計算量。
papers.title: Compute Where it Counts: Self Optimizing Language Models

primary_link.title: Likelihood scoring for continuations of mathematical text: a self-supervised benchmark with tests for shortcut vulnerabilities

radar.summary: We introduce an automatically generated benchmark for predicting hidden text in technical papers. A paper supplies visible context $X$ and a hidden continuation $Y$; the evaluated model writes an auxiliary forecast string $Z$, and a separate scorer assigns next-token probability to $Y$ both with and without conditioning on $Z$. This gives a label-free test of whether $Z$ transmits information about the continuation, compared against controls where $Z$ is recent context rather than a forecast. Ou
radar.summary_zh: 我們引入了一個自動生成的 benchmark，用於預測技術論文中的隱藏文本。一篇論文提供可見上下文 $X$ 和隱藏延續 $Y$；被評估的模型寫出一個輔助預測字串 $Z$，一個單獨的評分器在有和沒有條件 $Z$ 的情況下，為 $Y$ 分配 next-token probability。這提供了一個無標籤測試，檢驗 $Z$ 是否傳遞了關於延續的資訊，並與 $Z$ 是近期上下文而非預測的控制組進行比較。
radar.title: Likelihood scoring for continuations of mathematical text: a self-supervised benchmark with tests for shortcut vulnerabilities
radar.title_zh: 數學文本延續的似然評分：一個帶有捷徑漏洞測試的 self-supervised benchmark

links.title: Introducing Claude Platform on AWS: Anthropic’s native platform, through your AWS account

primary_link.title: Introducing Claude Platform on AWS: Anthropic’s native platform, through your AWS account

radar.summary: Today, we're excited to announce the general availability of Claude Platform on AWS. Claude Platform on AWS is a new service that gives customers direct access to Anthropic's native Claude Platform experience through their AWS account, with no separate credentials, contracts, or billing relationships required. AWS is the first cloud provider to offer access to the native Claude Platform experience. In this post, we explore how Claude Platform on AWS works and how you can start using it today.
radar.title: Introducing Claude Platform on AWS: Anthropic’s native platform, through your AWS account

links.title: Manufacturing intelligence with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings

primary_link.title: Manufacturing intelligence with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings

radar.summary: In this post, we build a multimodal retrieval system for aerospace manufacturing documents using Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings on Amazon Bedrock and Amazon S3 Vectors. We evaluate the system on 26 manufacturing queries and compare generation quality between a text-only pipeline and the multimodal pipeline.
radar.title: Manufacturing intelligence with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings

radar.title: Building web search-enabled agents with Strands and Exa

authors: Philipp Pavlov, Dmytro Romantsov, Evgeny Mironenko, Gowri Suryanarayana

links.title: Phoenix-VL 1.5 Medium Technical Report

primary_link.title: Phoenix-VL 1.5 Medium Technical Report

top5.summary: We introduce Phoenix-VL 1.5 Medium, a 123B-parameter natively multimodal and multilingual foundation model, adapted to regional languages and the Singapore context. Developed as a sovereign AI asset, it demonstrates that deep domain adaptation can be achieved with minimal degradation to broad-spectrum intelligence and alignment. Continued pretraining was performed on Mistral Medium 3.1 using a localized 1-trillion tokens multimodal corpus, followed by a 250-billion tokens long-context extension
top5.summary_zh: 我們介紹了 Phoenix-VL 1.5 Medium，這是一個擁有 123B 參數的原生多模態及多語言基礎模型，專為區域語言和新加坡語境進行了調整。作為一個主權 AI 資產開發，它證明了在對廣泛智能和對齊的最小化損害下，可以實現深度領域適應。我們使用一個本地化的 1 兆 tokens 多模態語料庫，在 Mistral Medium 3.1 上進行了持續預訓練，隨後進行了 2500 億 tokens 的長上下文擴展。
top5.title: Phoenix-VL 1.5 Medium Technical Report

links.title: RelFlexformer: Efficient Attention 3D-Transformers for Integrable Relative Positional Encodings

They lead to the new class of 3D-Transformer models, called \textit{RelFlexformers}, flexibly integrating those RPEs, and characterized by the $O(L \log L)$ time complexity of the attention computation for the $L$-length input sequences. RelFlexformers builds on the theory of the Non-Uniform Fourier Transform (NU-FFT), n
top5.summary_zh: 我們提出了一類新的高效注意力機制，應用由任意可積分調變函數 $f$ 給出的通用 3D Relative Positional Encoding (RPE) 方法。這些方法催生了新型的 3D-Transformer 模型，稱為 \textit{RelFlexformers}，它們靈活地整合了這些 RPE，並以 $L$ 長度輸入序列的注意力計算時間複雜度為 $O(L \log L)$ 為特徵。RelFlexformers 建立在 Non-Uniform Fourier Transform (NU-FFT) 的理論基礎上。

links.title: ComplexMCP: Evaluation of LLM Agents in Dynamic, Interdependent, and Large-Scale Tool Sandbox

primary_link.title: ComplexMCP: Evaluation of LLM Agents in Dynamic, Interdependent, and Large-Scale Tool Sandbox

top5.summary: Current LLM agents are proficient at calling isolated APIs but struggle with the "last mile" of commercial software automation. In real-world scenarios, tools are not independent; they are atomic, interdependent, and prone to environmental noise. We introduce $\textbf{ComplexMCP}$, a benchmark designed to evaluate agents in these rigorous conditions. links.title: Unsupervised Process Reward Models

primary_link.title: Unsupervised Process Reward Models

top5.summary: Process Reward Models (PRMs) are a powerful mechanism for steering large language model reasoning by providing fine-grained, step-level supervision. However, this effectiveness comes at a significant cost: PRMs require expert annotations for every reasoning step, making them costly and difficult to scale. Here, we propose a method for training unsupervised PRMs (uPRM) that requires no human supervision, neither at the level of step-by-step annotations nor through ground-truth verification of fin
top5.summary_zh: Process Reward Models (PRMs) 是一種強大的機制，透過提供細粒度的步驟級監督來引導大型語言模型的推理。然而，這種有效性伴隨著巨大的成本：PRMs 需要對每個推理步驟進行專家註釋，這使得它們成本高昂且難以擴展。在此，我們提出了一種訓練無監督 PRMs (uPRM) 的方法，它不需要任何人工監督，無論是在逐步註釋層面，還是透過對最終結果的真實性驗證。
top5.title: Unsupervised Process Reward Models
